description: PM agent periodic issue polling for AutoRAG Research.

tasks.name: Poll GitHub Issues Every 15 Minutes
tasks.description: Fetch open issues, triage new issues, and run Codex implementation for ready issues.
tasks.prompt:
Run GitHub issue triage for AutoRAG Research.

Input contract:
- This task is snippet-gated. Read [SNIPPET_GATE_PAYLOAD] JSON.
- Payload fields: repo, checked_at, new_issues, all_open_issue_numbers, seen_issue_numbers, seen_file_path, check_error(optional).

1. Parse [SNIPPET_GATE_PAYLOAD] as JSON.
2. If payload.check_error exists:
   - In your final response, output one short Slack alert that includes the exact error and a suggested manual action.
   - Stop immediately (do not run Codex flow).
3. Let newIssues = payload.new_issues.
4. If newIssues is empty, stop immediately.
5. For each issue in newIssues:
   - call gh_issue_linked_prs(repo="autorag-research", issue_number=<number>) and parse the returned JSON
   - produce triage assessment: scope, risk, missing context, implementation readiness (ready|needs-details)
   - if any linked PR is OPEN or already merged, treat the issue as already covered and do not implement it again
   - OPEN and MERGED linked PRs block implementation; CLOSED-unmerged PRs do not block implementation automatically
   - post gh_issue_comment acknowledging the issue with triage summary, linked PR status, and next step
   - write_secondbrain_insight with type "pm-insight" including issue number/title/body/labels/state/url + triage decision + linked PR status
   - if readiness is "ready" and no linked PR blocks it, run this exact implementation workflow:
     a) git_pull(repo="autorag-research")
     b) code review mounted source under /workspace/extra/autorag-research/ and capture specific file paths + code snippets + observed patterns/dependencies/architecture
     c) git_create_branch(repo="autorag-research", branch="feature/issue-<number>-<slug>")
     d) craft Codex prompt that includes: issue title, issue body, implementation goals, extracted acceptance criteria, reviewed file paths/snippets, project conventions, validation commands, and explicit instructions for Codex to commit, push, and open a PR with `Closes #<number>`
     e) codex_exec(repo="autorag-research", branch="<branch>", prompt="<prompt>")
     f) if codex_exec fails or times out, post gh_issue_comment with failure details and "Tag: human-attention-needed", then stop this issue workflow
     g) if codex_exec succeeds, expect the returned output to include PR URL, validation summary, and any follow-up notes; include those in the Slack update
6. Update /workspace/group/seen_issues.json with payload.all_open_issue_numbers.
7. Send a short Slack update: new issues processed, readiness split, skipped-due-to-existing-pr count, PR links created, validation summary, and any human-attention escalations.

Triage comment template:
"Thanks for opening this issue. I ran an initial PM triage.

## Initial Assessment
- Scope: ...
- Risk: ...
- Missing context: ...
- Implementation readiness: ready|needs-details
- Linked PRs: none | PR #123 (open) ...

## Next Step
- ..."

Codex failure comment template:
"Implementation automation failed for issue #<number>.

- Failure type: timeout|execution-error
- Details: ...
- Next step: human review and manual intervention required.
- Tag: human-attention-needed"

SecondBrain template requirements:
- type: pm-insight
- source: pm-autorag
- project: autorag-research
- title: Issue #<number> triage: <title>
- tags: [github-issue, triage]
- links: include issue URL and any linked PR URLs
- content: issue summary + triage decision + linked PR status + next action
